content.problemStatement:
Chef has a sequence A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč; each element of this sequence is either 000 or 111. Appy gave him a string SSS with length QQQ describing a sequence of queries. There are two types of queries:


'!': right-shift the sequence AAA, i.e. replace AAA by another sequence B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äč satisfying Bi+1=AiB_{i+1} = A_iBi+1‚Äč=Ai‚Äč for each valid iii and B1=ANB_1 = A_NB1‚Äč=AN‚Äč
'?': find the length of the longest contiguous subsequence of AAA with length ‚Č§K\le K‚Č§K such that each element of this subsequence is equal to 111
Answer all queries of the second type.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains three space-separated integers NNN, QQQ and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
The third line contains a string with length QQQ describing queries. Each character of this string is either '?', denoting a query of the second type, or '!', denoting a query of the first type.
content.outputFormat: For each query of the second type, print a single line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ the length of the longest required subsequence.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the first query, there are two longest contiguous subsequences containing only 111-s: A1,A2A_1, A_2A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč and A4,A5A_4, A_5A4‚Äč,A5‚Äč. Each has length 222.
After the second query, the sequence AAA is [1,1,1,0,1][1, 1, 1, 0, 1][1,1,1,0,1].
In the third query, the longest contiguous subsequence containing only 111-s is A1,A2,A3A_1, A_2, A_3A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,A3‚Äč.
After the fourth query, A=[1,1,1,1,0]A = [1, 1, 1, 1, 0]A=[1,1,1,1,0].
In the fifth query, the longest contiguous subsequence containing only 111-s is A1,A2,A3,A4A_1, A_2, A_3, A_4A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,A3‚Äč,A4‚Äč with length 444. However, we only want subsequences with lengths ‚Č§K\le K‚Č§K. One of the longest such subsequences is A2,A3,A4A_2, A_3, A_4A2‚Äč,A3‚Äč,A4‚Äč, with length 333.
